spectral_signature_overlap: none. NLI-based forward-backward consistency is a purely semantic/symbolic method. No eigenvalues, spectral entropy, frequency-domain analysis, or entropy trajectories are involved. The method checks whether a reasoning chain entails its explanation and vice versa — this is a logical consistency check, not a signal-processing approach. Zero overlap with our spectral decomposition of H(n).

reported_value: AUROC 0.996 on multistep arithmetic (per brief description); 70% fewer tokens than Semantic Entropy
overlapping_benchmark: [uncertain] — multistep arithmetic overlaps conceptually with GSM8K/MATH-500 which we use
applicability: medium — strong baseline on math reasoning tasks; orthogonal method (NLI vs. spectral); token-efficient
preemption_risk: LOW. NLI-based forward-backward consistency (RES) is methodologically entirely different from our spectral entropy approach: (1) RES is a semantic/logical consistency check requiring a generated explanation — it needs an additional inference pass to produce an explanation. Our method uses only the original generation. (2) RES involves NLI model calls (DeBERTa or equivalent) — black-box or gray-box, no internal states required. Our method requires logit access. (3) RES does not model entropy trajectories, attention maps, or any temporal/spectral signal. (4) The claimed 0.996 AUROC on multistep arithmetic is extraordinary — if real, it suggests the benchmark is somewhat synthetic/controlled. Our MATH-500 result of 90.0% on a competitive benchmark is a more realistic comparison point. (5) The 70% token reduction claim implies RES is more efficient for some use cases but still requires generating an explanation. Our method adds zero extra tokens. Conclusion: RES is a strong baseline to cite and compare against, but it does not compete in our spectral feature contribution space.
bracha_alignment: High. NLI-based consistency checking (forward-backward entailment) generates a calibrated confidence score that is a natural input for Bracha's LTT/conformal calibration. Her COIN/PPI framework could extend RES to semi-supervised settings where explanation labels are partially available. The bidirectional NLI score is also amenable to conformal prediction intervals.
ofir_alignment: Low. RES is a semantic-symbolic method with no structural or spectral component. Ofir's spectral/multi-view kernel methods (VSDE/PRAE/STG) would not naturally extend RES. The connection would be indirect — using spectral features as additional views alongside NLI consistency scores in a multi-view fusion.

The 0.996 AUROC headline number is extraordinarily high — approach with caution until the full paper is verified. All fields marked [uncertain] should be updated once the actual paper is located.
